data: Wide, slightly irregular in size v-cut letters: alpha wide with straight bar; epsilon has shorter middle bar; omicron is full size; pi has equal length hastae; omega is rhomboid and almost closed; sigma has parallel top and bottom strokes, and slight tendency to curvature in line 2.
Contrada Cozzo Sisca, above the Tellaro river
Observed and photographed in museum, by Prag, 02.03.2023
Photo J. Prag 02.03.2023, with the permission of the Assessorato Regionale dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana - Dipartimento dei Beni Culturali e dell’Identità Siciliana

The museum was damaged in an earthquake and all the ancient archaeological material was sent to the Paolo Orsi for safe keeping. They have not yet been returned and the collection currently consists of stones from various medieval buildings, some medieval armour and weaponry, and 2 ancient inscribed blocks
Association pour l’encouragement des études greques, « Bulletin épigraphique », Revue des études grecques, 1888, http://www.worldcat.org/oclc/797735566
Inscriptions grecques dialectales de Sicile : contribution à l'étude du vocabulaire grec colonial
Laurent Dubois, Inscriptions grecques dialectales de Sicile : contribution à l’étude du vocabulaire grec colonial, CEFR 119 (Rome: Ecole française de Rome, 1989)
Ricerche di antichità e di epigrafia siceliote
Giacomo Manganaro, «Ricerche di antichità e di epigrafia siceliote», Archeologia Classica 17 (1965): 183–210
Noto greca e romana:fonti storiografiche,epigrafi e pseudo-monete

Giacomo Manganaro, «Noto greca e romana:fonti storiografiche,epigrafi e pseudo-monete», in Contributi alla geografia storica dell’agro netino. Atti delle Giornate di Studio. Noto, Palazzo Trigona, 29-31 maggio 1998, a c. di F. Balsamo e V. La Rosa (Rosolini, 2001), 73–96

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag
1Ἀγέμαχος (vac.1)Πολυάρχου
2Ἐλώρωι καὶ (vac.1)Τιμάσσαι

Text encoding: Jonathan Prag
1ΑΓΕΜΑΧΟΣ    ΠΟΛΥΑΡΧΟΥ
2ΕΛΩΡΩΙΚΑΙ    ΤΙΜΑΣΣΑΙ

Facsimile Images

Apparatus criticus

  • Text of Manganaro 1965 checked against photograph

English translation

Jonathan Prag

Agemachos son of Polymarchos (dedicated this) to Eloros and Timassa

commentary

A dedication, to Eloros, presumably the local river god, and to Timassa, perhaps a local nymph (?). Manganaro notes that there are scratched letters/traces visible at the end of the inscription, assumed to be later additions. The letter forms, such as the rhomboid omega, find second-century parallels, for instance, at Segesta.

The letter forms, such as the rhomboid omega, find second-century parallels, for instance, at Segesta.
